title: DNA extraction from apple
author: Anthony Venon
materials: Macherey-Nagel NucleoSpin Plant II DNA extraction kit

steps.title: We collected apple buds and apple...
steps.instruction: We collected apple buds and apple leaves in the field in 2mL tubes with stainless steel balls. The samples were then flash-frozen and stored at -80°C for long-term conservation.

steps.title: Prepare 400 µL Carlson Lysis buffer per sample
steps.instruction: Prepare 400 µL Carlson Lysis buffer per sample: 100 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl pH 9.5 , 2 Mass / % volume CTAB, 1.4 Molarity (M) NaCl 1 Mass / % volume PEG 8000, 20 millimolar (mM) EDTA. The buffer can be prepared in advance by batches of 1 or 2L. Prepare the buffer at least one day in advance.

steps.title: Place the frozen samples into pre-cooled...
steps.instruction: Place the frozen samples into pre-cooled metal adapters compatible with the Qiagen TissueLyser II or III.

steps.title: First homogenization round
steps.instruction: First homogenization round: Run the TissueLyser for 00:00:30 at 22 Hz.

steps.title: Immediately after the first run, immerse...
steps.instruction: Immediately after the first run, immerse both the tube and the metal adapter in liquid nitrogen to re-freeze the sample and maintain cryogenic conditions.

steps.title: ⚠️ Note
steps.instruction: ⚠️ Note: If you are processing many samples or working slowly, it may be necessary to refresh the metal adapters in liquid nitrogen between runs to ensure optimal freezing.

steps.title: Second homogenization round
steps.instruction: Second homogenization round: Run the TissueLyser again for 00:00:30 at 22 Hz.

steps.title: After the second homogenization, immediately add...
steps.instruction: After the second homogenization, immediately add 400 µL of Carlson lysis buffer to each sample tube.

steps.title: Add 10 µL of RNase A...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of RNase A (provided with the Macherey-Nagel NucleoSpin Plant II DNA extraction kit ) to each tube.

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting up and down or briefly vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting up and down or briefly vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate the samples at 65 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples at 65 °C for 00:15:00 to facilitate cell lysis and improve DNA yield.

steps.title: Working under a chemical fume hood,...
steps.instruction: Working under a chemical fume hood, add 400 µL of chloroform to each lysed sample tube.

steps.title: Vortex the samples vigorously for 00:00:15...
steps.instruction: Vortex the samples vigorously for 00:00:15 to ensure complete mixing of the aqueous and organic phases.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at 11000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at 11000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes to separate the phases.

steps.title: While centrifuging, prepare the silica spin...
steps.instruction: While centrifuging, prepare the silica spin columns (green) by placing each one into a collection tube.

steps.title: In a separate empty collection tube...
steps.instruction: In a separate empty collection tube (without a column), add 450 µL of PC buffer (provided in the Macherey-Nagel kit).

steps.title: After centrifugation, carefully collect the upper...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, carefully collect the upper aqueous phase (avoid disturbing the interphase and organic layer).

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant into the tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant into the tube containing 450 µL of PC buffer.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down several times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down several times.

steps.title: Load 700 µL of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Load 700 µL of the mixture onto the green silica spin column placed in its collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the silica column at 11000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the silica column at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .

steps.title: Discard the flow-through from the collection...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through from the collection tube and place the column back into the same tube.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of PW1 buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of PW1 buffer to the column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and return the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and return the column to the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 650 µL of PW2 buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 650 µL of PW2 buffer to the column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and return the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and return the column to the collection tube.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of PW2 buffer again to the column
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of PW2 buffer again to the column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes to ensure complete removal of residual ethanol.

steps.title: Discard the collection tube and transfer...
steps.instruction: Discard the collection tube and transfer the column to a new, clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 50 µL of pre-warmed elution...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of pre-warmed elution buffer (PE buffer) directly to the center of the column membrane. Buffer should be preheated to 55 °C .

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes to allow elution
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes to allow elution.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute to collect the eluted DNA.

steps.title: (Optional second elution)
steps.instruction: (Optional second elution): Pipette the eluate back onto the column membrane.

steps.title: Wait an additional 5 minutes, then...
steps.instruction: Wait an additional 5 minutes, then centrifuge again at 11000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .

steps.title: Discard the column, and keep the...
steps.instruction: Discard the column, and keep the eluate in the microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Use a Nanodrop spectrophotometer and/or Qubit...
steps.instruction: Use a Nanodrop spectrophotometer and/or Qubit fluorometer to measure DNA concentration and assess purity.

steps.title: %s %s
steps.instruction: %s %s

steps.title: Summary of ONT sequencing run (position...
steps.instruction: Summary of ONT sequencing run (position A, ES_A23_b; ES_C28_b; ES_E38_b; FR_C12_a samples) Run performed on a FLO-PRO114M flow cell using the PromethION 2 Solo (P2-Solo) with 4 multiplexed samples (ES_A23_b; ES_C28_b; ES_E38_b; FR_C12_a). A total of 139.63 Gb of sequence data was generated over 72 hours , with 34.11 million reads . Basecalling success reached 100% , with 126.77 Gb of high-quality reads (Q ≥ 8). The read N50 was 12.13 kb. Figure

steps.title: Read length distribution and outlier analysis...
steps.instruction: Read length distribution and outlier analysis (position A, ES_A23_b; ES_C28_b; ES_E38_b; FR_C12_a samples) The read length histogram for run shows a peak between 3–10 kb, with a long tail extending beyond 40 kb. The N50 of 12.13 kb confirms a robust proportion of long reads. Outlier analysis reveals several ultra-long reads exceeding 49 kb. Figure

steps.title: Summary of ONT sequencing run (position...
steps.instruction: Summary of ONT sequencing run (position B, FR_E28_a; FR_E18_b; FR_A4_a; ES_C24_b samples) Run performed on the same PromethION 2 Solo device using a second flow cell (FLO-PRO114M), loaded with 4 multiplexed (FR_E28_a; FR_E18_b; FR_A4_a; ES_C24_b). The run yielded 120.09 Gb of sequence data across 71 hours 59 minutes , totaling 28.7 million reads . Of those, 25.37 million reads (106.25 Gb) passed the Q8 quality threshold. The N50 was 11.88 kb , comparable to run A and suitable for downstream long-read analysis. Figure

steps.title: Read length distribution and outlier analysis...
steps.instruction: Read length distribution and outlier analysis (position B, FR_E28_a; FR_E18_b; FR_A4_a; ES_C24_b samples) Read lengths for run P2S-02916-B showed a distribution similar to run A, with a peak between 3–10 kb and an N50 of 11.88 kb .
